channel.title: Chearleader Pom Poms[Seeking Greenlight]
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Chearleader Pom Poms[Seeking Greenlight]" - Cheerleader Pom Poms that make everybody near you happy.

item.title: Forum Criticism Team - Captain Post
item.content:encoded: VoxStulti, if you don't meet the criteria to give greenlights, please don't confuse authors by posting "non-official greenlights". Given that staff have encountered authors who have mistakenly thought that any review given counted as a greenlight (even if the reviewer gave a negative review), the less confusion in the forums, the better.

item.title: Re: Chearleader Pom Poms[Seeking Greenlight]
item.content:encoded:
Response:
+ show block
– hide block

what if I use Sage Evergreen idea that before the pom poms article was being typed the pom-poms started influencing everybody in the site because they didn't realize that the effects kept going even when the pom poms are taken off? or maybe a containment breach that would cause its waves to influence the whole site? So when the person typing the article made up a description because he didn't bother with reading the actual description so he skips right to the recordings until the very end when he actually types the real one in?

item.title: Re: Chearleader Pom Poms[Seeking Greenlight]
item.content:encoded:
That is true. Forgot about that part of the effect. Well I think that clears up that part of my commentary. Consider this a NON-OFFICIAL Greenlight. I think you have a good idea here and with a little ironing out of the narrative details addressed by the others, I think you could have a great idea. Hopefully i'll get a chance to read it one day.

- Vox

item.title: Re: Chearleader Pom Poms[Seeking Greenlight]
item.content:encoded: Thanks by the way in the additional notes it says the effects happen till there are no more memories to be erased even if you take the pom poms off. thanks for the nonofficial greenlight now I can go to the nonofficial draft section

item.title: Re: Chearleader Pom Poms[Seeking Greenlight]
item.content:encoded:
Hello my thoughts exactly I was going to change the order since I wasn't 100% sure I could change the order of sections

You, technically can in that there's no rules against it; but it's a real bad idea to do so just to actually tell this story well.

How about I show how much happiness she had by showing everybody dies in the town she is in from malnutrition?

This doesn't change the core issue; which is that the progression of the article is eminently predictable as soon as we've finished reading the description. What you really need to do is to figure out a subversion of the predictable somehow.

item.title: Re: Chearleader Pom Poms[Seeking Greenlight]
item.content:encoded:
Howdy fluffal,
Flattered by the PM. If you saw me in another thread you may have seen my disclaimer, but I am not an official reviewer. So:
A. I cannot greenlight unfortunately
B. Take my feedback with a grain of salt compared to that of real reviewers like cybersquid & Dr Sage Evergreen.

Nevertheless, lets dig in.

Click to dig in
Click to un-dig in

As you may have noticed, you have a lot of support from some veterans here; so congrats. And I agree: I like the idea, I like the narrative, but execution is important to not spoil the surprise. Now parroting the others doesn't do you much good, so here is my thoughts. A possible means of execution is to make the depressive aspects of the object less noticeable, as in during initial testing on the object the foundation only observed the euphoric effects on others. But after acquisition of the tape, and addenda is made and the description is expanded upon. This idea may be up a similar path as Vivarium's but figured i'd add my own clarification. I also like Evergreen's proposal as well. Either way, I think you have a great idea here. My only other question is what compels Emily to continue using the pom-poms. While yes, I doubt that most people would jump to the conclusion that their cheer gear is the cause of their misery, many (and I am of course generalizing here), but many individuals suffering depression express decreased motivation in the things they love. Would that not have happened to her? I'm not saying that there has to be an anomalous explanation for this, but keep this question in mind while further weaving your narrative.

Hopefully I could be some help; Best of luck,
VoxStulti

item.title: Re: Chearleader Pom Poms[Seeking Greenlight]
item.content:encoded:
[Summoned via PM]

Hi there! Disclaimer I'm not a veteran reviewer and I'm unable to provide Greenlights. Without further ado…

Lets take a look at this.
-

It looks like I will be echoing the same points as Dr Sage Evergreen because I like this a lot. I am a big fan of emotional articles like this, and I think this is a very well thought out idea. The most important thing to keep in mind with this article will be execution. Like cybersqyd said, you will need to be careful that you do not give away the ending too early on. You could solve this with Dr Sage Evergreen's idea, and a good example of an article that tells a story in this way would be SCP-5866. It is told from a viewpoint of an article that is in the process of being written. You could do something similar to this, perhaps having a separate researcher re editing the file in order to fix his mistakes, and slowly finding out what happened through the course of the edits. I honestly wish I could greenlight this, but sadly I can't. That is why I recommend going over to the IRC chatrom, and going over to #thecritters. You can post a link to your forum page there, and this will help you find your final greenlight.

To summarize, I like this idea a lot, and I wish you luck going forward. I hope I see this in the Draft Forum soon.

item.title: Re: Chearleader Pom Poms[Seeking Greenlight]
item.content:encoded:
Hello Firstly, I just wanted to say I love how you start with your introduction to the critique yeah thanks for the suggestion I will try to do it I might need help with making the 4-5 descriptions though so if you could help me maybe some pointers on what they should or shouldn't have that would be cool.

P.S thanks so much for the greenlight.

item.title: Re: Chearleader Pom Poms[Seeking Greenlight]
item.content:encoded:
Summoned via pm
Hello there!
Interesting SCP you've got here…
Would be a shame if someone…
Critiqued it…

+ show block
– hide block

So, I'm going to be honest, I love this idea. I really, really like the story, the characters, the anomaly, all that good stuff, it's perfect. So I'm going to go ahead and give you a greenlight.
However, I do have some of concerns I'd like you to take care of before going through with your idea. This is the same concern that Cybersqyd pointed out. The story is predictable, primarily because we know about the affects of the anomaly. So, the way that I'm going to suggest that you overcome this is by leaving the actual description towards the end. Maybe have the person writing the article be affected by the waves? They keep writing the wrong things? Or going off topic? Perhaps there can be 4-5 different descriptions between the addendums, and at the very end of your paper is an actual description of the anomalous properties, with an ominous ending indicating that the SCP has reeked some sort of unknown havoc onto the foundation.
Please do reach out if you have any questions about the story line/article! And keep me posted on how the article turns out. You can reach me by pm if you need help. Best of luck with the article!

item.title: Re: Chearleader Pom Poms[Seeking Greenlight]
item.content:encoded: On a rock or a tripod; just so long as you catch her jumping into the water. The parents dying from malnutrition would be sort of interesting. That could actually be sort of interesting.

item.title: Re: Chearleader Pom Poms[Seeking Greenlight]
item.content:encoded: Hello my thoughts exactly I was going to change the order since I wasn't 100% sure I could change the order of sections , but I was going to do it in the draft section. How about I show how much happiness she had by showing everybody dies in the town she is in from malnutrition?

item.title: Re: Chearleader Pom Poms[Seeking Greenlight]
item.content:encoded: Thanks for the critique yeah I don't really like the idol issue much either I have already seen it one too many times. I was planning for her to leave it on the side of the lake right next to a rock. Should I make the parents die from malnutrition since they are so happy they can't control themselves, and maybe she used to have so much happiness it affected the whole town to die from being too happy? just a thought. Yeah, I suppose I could put it in a tripod.

item.title: Re: Chearleader Pom Poms[Seeking Greenlight]
item.content:encoded:
My core concern with this as a SCP article is that whilst the central narrative section definitely feels emotional and hard hitting in the right way, it also feels like the actual story being told is eminently predictable as soon as the characters are introduced, especially considering the description of what the pom poms do is gonna be before the addenda containing the narrative. Indeed, even the description alone is enough to chart out the story: the actual progression just, does exactly what you would expect from a thing that sucks the happiness out a person and sends it to everyone around them.

My advice here would be to consider how you can make this land better. Perhaps it'd be by making this a tale told from the perspective of the webcam logs where the behaviour of the pom poms is deliberately being hidden from the reader; perhaps it's by subverting the narrative path somehow, whether by having someone intervene at the final moment or by like, really showing the effect too much happiness is having on the family too and having that be almost more of the focus or something else.

item.title: Re: Chearleader Pom Poms[Seeking Greenlight]
item.content:encoded:
1) You did fine on the waves. I hate saying it's all pseudo-science anyway and we expect some hand-waving, but you did fine.

2) This is a pretty good SCP. You have the basic something that has to come from somewhere trope, and it feels pretty good here instead of cliched. In that regard, kudos.

3) If you end up writing it up, try to avoid the idol issue: The girl becomes more popular the sadder she gets. While it makes sense, especially as she makes people feel better so it's only natural that she draws people in, you want to keep it where it's at, where the others become more interested in their happiness and ignore the cause of it.

4) If you want to up the drama, put the webcam on a tripod at the end so we see her falling into the river. Not only does it provide certainty of her fate, but it also makes a great capstone to the tale. You may also want to mention to whom she's giving the pom-poms just to keep the terror going. However, keep in mind that The Foundation has to grab them at some point, so you need to keep in mind that there has to be some way for them to get involved.

Otherwise, great idea and follow-through!

item.title: Chearleader Pom Poms[Seeking Greenlight]
item.content:encoded:
Seeking Greenlights: Yes

Page Type: SCP Article

Elevator Pitch: SCP-XXXX is a pair of Pom Poms each having identical red, white, and blue stripes they don't display any signs of considerable wear. When a human holds SCP-XXXX, hereafter designed SCP-XXXX-1, will have their memories erased that caused them strong euphoria, this process can take only a few minutes or hours depending on the number of memories. When SCP-XXXX-1 has no more memories that can be extracted they will start to emit waves, when these waves contact a human will cause an immediate euphoria, the amount of euphoria, the length of the waves, and how long they experience euphoria is dependent on how many memories were erased to SCP-XXXX-1.
+ show block
– hide block

Central Narrative: The central narrative of this piece would take the form of the recordings of a Webcam taking place in a high school with two sisters named Lyvia, and Emily. All recordings were made by Lyvia until the last recording. The first recording shows the discovery of SCP-XXXX when they join a cheerleading club. Over time the recordings show the increasing depression of Emily. Almost all of the recordings show the parents of Emily and Lyvia being filled with happiness ignoring the well-being of Emily this resulted in Emily isolating herself to a nearby lake. The last recording shows Emily facing a lake repeating these questions: Who are they? Where am I? Why can't I feel anything? Why is everybody so happy?. This recording lasts until a faint sound of a splash due to the webcam being water damaged though the memory chip was unaffected.

Hook/Attention Grabber: The primary hook of this article is the way it shows how it affects SCP-XXXX-1, and the people near them. As people read the recordings they will feel more attached once it shows the connection between Emily with her family. The Pom Poms are based on a simple concept(Pom Poms that make you sadder, and make people near you happier)but gets more tragic when it goes more in-depth on its discovery.

Additional Notes:
The effects on SCP-XXXX-1 last until there are no memories to use as waves, or when they die.
